heading: Demand on postmaster
label: 1743 Demand on postmaster
text: The certificate of the Postmaster General or the Government Accountability Office of the mailing to a postmaster of a statement of his account and that payment of the balance stated has not been received shall be sufficient evidence of a demand notwithstanding any allowances or credits subsequently made. A copy of such statement shall be attached to the certificate.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 670 (R.S. § 890; , ).June 10, 1921, ch. 18, § 30142 Stat. 23Provisions in , U.S.C., 1940 ed., that the statement should recite that a letter has been mailed to a described post office and sufficient time has elapsed for it to have reached its destination, was omitted as superfluous.section 670 of title 28The last clause of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., was omitted as covered by the phrase “notwithstanding any allowances or credits subsequently made” in the revised section.section 670 of title 28Changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments2004— substituted “Government Accountability Office” for “General Accounting Office”.Pub. L. 108–271

Transfer of FunctionsThe office of Postmaster General of the Post Office Department was abolished and all functions, powers, and duties of the Postmaster General were transferred to the United States Postal Service by , , , set out as a note under , Postal Service.Pub. L. 91–375, § 4(a)84 Stat. 773section 201 of Title 39Aug. 12, 1970
